lede: A look at January 25 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Vladimir Vysotsky and Corazon Aquino, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Ethiopian Airlines Flight 409
events.desc: Ethiopian Airlines Flight 409 crashes into the Mediterranean Sea off the coast of Na'ameh, Lebanon, killing 90.

events.title: Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam
events.desc: A suicide attack by the Liberation Tigers of Tamil Eelam on Sri Lanka's Temple of the Tooth kills eight and injures 25 others.

events.title: Pope John Paul II
events.desc: During a historic visit to Cuba, Pope John Paul II demands political reforms and the release of political prisoners while condemning US attempts to isolate the country.

events.title: Avianca Flight 052
events.desc: Avianca Flight 052 crashes in Cove Neck, New York, killing 73.

events.title: Idi Amin
events.desc: Idi Amin leads a coup deposing Milton Obote and becomes Uganda's president.

events.title: South Vietnam
events.desc: South Vietnamese junta leader and Prime Minister Nguyen Cao Ky fires rival, Deputy Prime Minister and Defence Minister Nguyen Huu Co, while the latter is overseas on a diplomatic visit.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Thailand declares war on the United States and United Kingdom.

events.title: Pope Pius XII
events.desc: Pope Pius XII elevates the Apostolic Vicariate of the Hawaiian Islands to the dignity of a diocese. It becomes the Roman Catholic Diocese of Honolulu.

events.title: Nellie Bly
events.desc: Nellie Bly completes her round-the-world journey in 72 days.

events.title: São Paulo
events.desc: São Paulo, Brazil, is founded by Jesuit priests.

births.name: Corazon Aquino
births.line: Filipino politician, 11th President of the Philippines

births.name: Virginia Woolf
births.line: English novelist, essayist, short story writer, and critic

deaths.text: Mary Tyler Moore, American actress and producer
Emperor Yingzong of Song, Emperor of China from 1063 to 1067
Gregory of Nazianzus, theologian and Patriarch of Constantinople

observances.text: Christian feast day: Feast of the Conversion of Saint Paul (Eastern Orthodox, Oriental Orthodox, Roman Catholic, Anglican and Lutheran churches, which concludes the Week of Prayer for Christian Unity)
Christian feast day: Gregory the Theologian (Eastern (Byzantine) Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: The last day of the Week of Prayer for Christian Unity (Christian ecumenism)
